slides.title: Pick Any Character from a String
slides.body: A string is like a row of numbered seats — every character has a **position**. Use **indexing** to grab one character, or **slicing** to grab a whole chunk.
slides.tip: These skills work on lists too — learn once, use everywhere.

slides.title: Positions Start at Zero
slides.body: Python numbers positions from **0**, not 1. The first character is `s[0]`, the second is `s[1]`, and so on.
slides.code:
word = "Python"
print(word[0])  # first character
print(word[2])  # third character
slides.tip: Think of it like a building where the ground floor is floor 0.

slides.title: Count Backwards with Negatives
slides.body: Use **negative indexes** to count from the end. `-1` is always the last character — no need to know the length!
slides.code:
word = "Python"
print(word[-1])  # last character
print(word[-3])  # third from the end
slides.tip: word[-1] is much handier than word[len(word)-1].

slides.title: Slicing: Grab a Range
slides.body: Use `s[start:stop]` to grab multiple characters at once. Python returns positions `start` up to — but **not including** — `stop`.
slides.code:
word = "Python"
print(word[0:3])  # positions 0, 1, 2
print(word[:3])   # same — omit start to begin at 0
print(word[2:])   # position 2 to the end
slides.tip: Omit start or stop and Python fills in the sensible default.

slides.title: Reverse a String with [::-1]
slides.body: Add a **step** as a third value: `s[start:stop:step]`. A step of `-1` walks backwards — making `s[::-1]` the classic way to reverse any string.
slides.code:
word = "Python"
print(word[::-1])
print(word[::2])   # every other character
slides.tip: Slices never crash with IndexError — Python just returns what it can.

slides.title: You Can Slice Anything Now!
slides.body: You now know how to grab **any character or chunk** from a string using `s[index]` and `s[start:stop:step]`. The same syntax works on lists — you already have a head start on the next topic!
slides.tip: Practice: try sentence[-3:] to grab the last 3 characters of any string.
